name: Duck with Sugar Cane (Vit Tiem Mia)
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Shelled peanuts, red skin removed
1/2 cup Chestnuts, either fresh or dried
1/2 cup Ginkgo nuts, canned or fresh
1 tbsp Finely chopped fresh ginger root
3 Scallions, both green part and white, cut into 4 pieces crosswise
Sprinkling of freshly ground black pepper
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the various nuts, lotus seeds, dried mushrooms, and red dates as follows:
- Soak peanuts in hot water for 30 minutes; drain and set aside.
- Boil dried chestnuts for 20 minutes; drain and set aside.
- Blanch fresh ginkgo nuts to remove the thin inner skin; set aside.
- Soak mushrooms in hot water for 20 minutes, then drain, remove stems, and cut into quarters.
- Boil lotus seeds for 20 minutes; drain and set aside.
- Soak red dates in hot water for 30 minutes (boil for 10 minutes if very dry); drain and set aside.
2. Rinse the duck, rub with salt, then let it dry for 10 minutes.
3. Combine ginger, shallots, and 1 teaspoon salt. Rub this mixture inside the duck.
4. Stuff the duck with peanuts, chestnuts, ginkgo nuts, lotus seeds, and 1 teaspoon salt.
Sew the duck closed with heavy white thread or skewers.
5. Peel sugar cane and cut into thin strips. Tie slices around the duck using kitchen string, covering it completely. Secure with additional ties as needed.
6. Place the duck in a large pot with enough water to cover it. Add coconut water, rock sugar, and 1 teaspoon salt. Bring to a boil, then remove scum continuously for 15 minutes. Reduce heat, keep bubbling, and simmer for 2 hours, turning the duck every 30 minutes.
7. Remove the duck; discard sugar cane. Add mushrooms, red dates, 1 teaspoon salt, and fish sauce to the pot. Boil, cover, and simmer for an additional 15 minutes. Transfer the duck to the broth.
8. Break the duck into pieces, place in bowls with some broth, nuts, red dates, and mushrooms, then garnish with black pepper and chopped coriander.
Makes 8 servings.
